qa_pairs.question: What is the consequence of a software release reaching its end-of-life (EOL)?
qa_pairs.answer: When a software release reaches its end-of-life (EOL), support and updates for it are no longer provided.

qa_pairs.question: What is the recommended action when a system's platform reaches its end-of-life?
qa_pairs.answer: When a platform reaches its end-of-life, it is encouraged to migrate systems and platforms to newer releases to continue receiving patches and security updates.

qa_pairs.question: What happens to older Linux distribution images once they are past their end-of-life?
qa_pairs.answer: Older images for Linux distributions past their end-of-life (EOL) will be removed.

qa_pairs.question: Can a user continue to run a virtual machine (VM) with a Linux distribution that has reached its end-of-life?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, users are not prevented from continuing to run a virtual machine (VM) with an EOL Linux distribution, though it is not recommended.

qa_pairs.question: Will images for Linux distributions past their end-of-life (EOL) be available for creating new virtual machines?
qa_pairs.answer: No, images for Linux distributions past their end-of-life (EOL) will no longer be available when creating new virtual machines (VMs).

qa_pairs.question: Why should users migrate to newer releases for their systems and platforms?
qa_pairs.answer: Users should migrate to newer releases to continue receiving patches and security updates, as releases past their end-of-life (EOL) no longer receive support or updates.
